title: Lipids in microalgae quantitation
author: Yingyu Hu
materials: Preparation of standard Glyceryl tripalmitate Contributed by users
Discard the remaining extract solution into waste tank.
Determine the subsampling method for the remaining replicates
For samples, subsample following the preliminary test.
For samples: Lipids_{ug/filter} =Lipids_{ug}*4/2 For blanks: Lipids_{ug/filter} =Lipids_{ug}*5/4.5

steps.title: Prepare glyceryl tripalmitate (GTP) primary standard solution (around 1 mg/ml)
steps.instruction: Prepare glyceryl tripalmitate (GTP) primary standard solution (around 1 mg/ml)

steps.title: Place frozen GTP in vacuum desiccator...
steps.instruction: Place frozen GTP in vacuum desiccator with lose cap until it is warmed to Room temperature before making primary standard solution

steps.title: Prepare working standards:
steps.instruction: Prepare working standards:

steps.title: 5 ug/vial
steps.instruction: 5 ug/vial: In a 12 ml clear vial, add 5 µL GTP primary standard to each vial.

steps.title: 10 ug/vial
steps.instruction: 10 ug/vial: In a 12 ml clear vial, add 10 µL GTP primary standard to each vial.

steps.title: 20 ug/vial
steps.instruction: 20 ug/vial: In a 12 ml clear vial, add 20 µL GTP primary standard to each vial.

steps.title: 40 ug/vial
steps.instruction: 40 ug/vial: In two 12 ml amber vials, add 40 µL GTP primary standard to each vial.

steps.title: 80 ug/vial In a 12 ml...
steps.instruction: 80 ug/vial In a 12 ml clear vial, add 80 µL GTP primary standard to each vial.

steps.title: Dry working standards at Room temperature...
steps.instruction: Dry working standards at Room temperature under N 2 gas stream (<2 psi).

steps.title: Estimate the total volume of potassium dichromate required
steps.instruction: Estimate the total volume of potassium dichromate required: Number of standards and standard blanks: 6 Number of sample blanks and samples using 0.2 mL dichromate: N Number of samples using 0.5 mL dichromate: M V=0.5*(6+M)+0.2*N

steps.title: Blank The process of redissolving and...
steps.instruction: Blank The process of redissolving and subsampling is essential for purification purposes, aimed at removing water-soluble organic substances such as proteins, amino acids, sugars, RNA, and DNA, which could potentially interfere with the dichromate assay.

steps.title: Add 5 mL chloroform into the...
steps.instruction: Add 5 mL chloroform into the dried extract Chloroform (HPLC grade) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #439142-4L

steps.title: Transfer 4
steps.instruction: Transfer 4.5 mL into a new vial.

steps.title: For microalgae samples that have been...
steps.instruction: For microalgae samples that have been rarely studied with no available information on lipid content, it is essential to conduct a preliminary test before processing all samples.

steps.title: Dry this 4
steps.instruction: Dry this 4.5 mL extract solution for assay.

steps.title: Add 4 mL chloroform into the dried sample extract
steps.instruction: Add 4 mL chloroform into the dried sample extract.

steps.title: Transfer 2 mL extract into another new vial
steps.instruction: Transfer 2 mL extract into another new vial.

steps.title: Dry the extract in the new vial for assay
steps.instruction: Dry the extract in the new vial for assay.

steps.title: Store the remaining extract -80 °C (for phospholipids assay)
steps.instruction: Store the remaining extract -80 °C (for phospholipids assay).

steps.title: Place frozen glyceryl tripalmitate (GTP) in...
steps.instruction: Place frozen glyceryl tripalmitate (GTP) in vacuum desiccator with lose cap until it is warmed to Room temperature before making primary standard solution

steps.title: Weigh and transfer GTP (around 2...
steps.instruction: Weigh and transfer GTP (around 2 mg ) to a clear vial , log the actual weight. GTP (mg) 1

steps.title: Dissolve GTP by chloroform for a...
steps.instruction: Dissolve GTP by chloroform for a concentration of about 1 mg/mL, gently vortex. chloroform (mL) 1

steps.title: 5 ug/vial
steps.instruction: 5 ug/vial: In a 12 ml clear vial, add 5 µL GTP primary standard to each vial.

steps.title: 10 ug/vial
steps.instruction: 10 ug/vial: In a 12 ml clear vial, add 10 µL GTP primary standard to each vial.

steps.title: 20 ug/vial
steps.instruction: 20 ug/vial: In a 12 ml clear vial, add 20 µL GTP primary standard to each vial.

steps.title: 40 ug/vial
steps.instruction: 40 ug/vial: In a 12 ml clear vial, add 40 µL GTP primary standard to each vial.

steps.title: 80 ug/vial In a 12 ml...
steps.instruction: 80 ug/vial In a 12 ml clear vial, add 80 µL GTP primary standard to each vial.

steps.title: 100 ug/vial In a 12 ml...
steps.instruction: 100 ug/vial In a 12 ml clear vial, add 100 µL GTP primary standard to each vial.

steps.title: The following steps require a 100 uL glass syringe
steps.instruction: The following steps require a 100 uL glass syringe: the syringe barrel and needle is chloroform rinsed and air-dried Gastight® 1700 Series Syringes (1710N, Hamilton, 81000)

steps.title: 120 ug/vial In a 12 ml...
steps.instruction: 120 ug/vial In a 12 ml clear vial, add 2X 60 µL GTP primary standard to each vial.

steps.title: 160 ug/vial In a 12 ml...
steps.instruction: 160 ug/vial In a 12 ml clear vial, add 2X 80 µL GTP primary standard to each vial.

steps.title: 200 ug/vial In a 12 ml...
steps.instruction: 200 ug/vial In a 12 ml clear vial, add 2X 100 µL GTP primary standard to each vial.

steps.title: 240 ug/vial In a 12 ml...
steps.instruction: 240 ug/vial In a 12 ml clear vial, add 3X 80 µL GTP primary standard to each vial.

steps.title: 300 ug/vial In a 12 ml...
steps.instruction: 300 ug/vial In a 12 ml clear vial, add 3X 100 µL GTP primary standard to each vial.

steps.title: Weigh and transfer GTP (around 1...
steps.instruction: Weigh and transfer GTP (around 1 mg ) to a clear vial , log the actual weight. GTP (mg) 1

steps.title: Dissolve GTP by chloroform for a...
steps.instruction: Dissolve GTP by chloroform for a concentration of about 1 mg/mL, gently vortex. chloroform (mL) 1

steps.title: Estimate the total volume of potassium dichromate required
steps.instruction: Estimate the total volume of potassium dichromate required: Number of standards and standard blanks: 12+2 Number of samples and sample blanks: N V=0.5*14+0.2*N

steps.title: Transfer concentrated sulfuric acid from the...
steps.instruction: Transfer concentrated sulfuric acid from the original package to a 95% ethanol rinsed and air-dried glass reagent bottle for temporary storage. Concentrated sulphuric acid Contributed by users

steps.title: Weigh a 95% ethanol rinsed and...
steps.instruction: Weigh a 95% ethanol rinsed and air-dried glass reagent bottle, and tare the balance

steps.title: Use 5 ml serological pipet to...
steps.instruction: Use 5 ml serological pipet to measure and transfer concentrated sulfuric acid to this vial. The volume of sulfuric acid is several milliliter more than estimated in go to step #6 . Write down the weight of sulfuric acid. Sulfuric acid (mL) Sulfuric acid (g) 1

steps.title: The weight of dichromate required for the 0
steps.instruction: The weight of dichromate required for the 0.15% (w/w) acid-dichromate reagent equals the weight of sulfuric acid multiplied by (0.15/99.85). Potassium dichromate Fisher Scientific Catalog #P188-100 (CAS: 7778-50-9) Dichromate (g) 1

steps.title: Weigh dichromate and dissolve it into concentrated sulfuric acid
steps.instruction: Weigh dichromate and dissolve it into concentrated sulfuric acid. Cap the vial and vortex until complete dissolve.

steps.title: Label two 12 mL vials with...
steps.instruction: Label two 12 mL vials with “+ Blank” and “- Blank”. "-Blank" is 0 ug GTP. "+Blank" is the reference of absorbance.

steps.title: Prepare boiling water bath on hot...
steps.instruction: Prepare boiling water bath on hot plate, place a vial rack in the water bath

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.5 mL of acid-dichromate reagent to \textcolor{red}{standards, +Blank, –Blank} . Cap and vortex right after. Use 5 mL glass serological pipet, fill to "4 mL", dispense 0.5 mL .

steps.title: Keep reaction vials in boiling water for 00:15:00
steps.instruction: Keep reaction vials in boiling water for 00:15:00 .

steps.title: Cool vials to Room temperature in the fumehood
steps.instruction: Cool vials to Room temperature in the fumehood

steps.title: Prepare 0
steps.instruction: Prepare 0.2 g/ml sodium sulphite solution Sodium sulphite Contributed by users (CAS: 7757-83-7) Weigh 0.2 g sodium sulphite in a 2 ml microtube. Add 1 mL MilliQ water into the tube. Vortex

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.125 mL o MilliQ (1 mL + 125 uL by pipet) to \textcolor{red}{standards, +Blank, –Blank} . Cap immediately and vortex.

steps.title: Cool vials to room temperature
steps.instruction: Cool vials to room temperature.

steps.title: Add 25 µL 0
steps.instruction: Add 25 µL 0.2 g/ml sodium sulphite solution to the “+Blank” vial. Vortex.

steps.title: Reverse pipetting, load 200 µL r reactant into microtiter plate
steps.instruction: Reverse pipetting, load 200 µL r reactant into microtiter plate.

steps.title: Subtract absorbance of "+Blank" from the absorbance of standards
steps.instruction: Subtract absorbance of "+Blank" from the absorbance of standards.

steps.title: Plot the resulted absorbance versus mass of GTP (ug)
steps.instruction: Plot the resulted absorbance versus mass of GTP (ug). Take notes of the color for using the corresponding curve. Concentration of lipids (left to right): 5 ug, 10 ug, 100 ug, 200 ug and 300 ug

steps.title: Calculate the mass of lipids by...
steps.instruction: Calculate the mass of lipids by using the standard curve and the absorbance, including sample blanks and samples. GTP_{ug} =(Abs-intercept)/slope

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.2 mL of acid-dichromate reagent to \textcolor{red}{samples, sample} \textcolor{red}{blanks} . Cap and vortex right after. Use 5 mL glass serological pipet, fill to "4 mL", dispense 0.2 mL .

steps.title: Add 450 µL MilliQ to \textcolor{red}{samples, sample} \textcolor{red}{blanks}
steps.instruction: Add 450 µL MilliQ to \textcolor{red}{samples, sample} \textcolor{red}{blanks} . Cap immediately and vortex.

steps.title: Preliminary result of GTP_{ug} is between...
steps.instruction: Preliminary result of GTP_{ug} is between 20 to 80 ug Use the same method to subsample and assay

steps.title: Preliminary result of GTP_{ug} is above...
steps.instruction: Preliminary result of GTP_{ug} is above 80 ug Use smaller portion from the extract or use 0.5 mL dichromate for an estimated GTP_{ug} value between 20 to 80 ug

steps.title: Preliminary result of GTP_{ug} is lower...
steps.instruction: Preliminary result of GTP_{ug} is lower than 20 ug Use larger portion from the extract and use 0.2 mL dichromate for an estimated GTP_{ug} value between 20 to 80 ug

steps.title: Dry working standards at Room temperature...
steps.instruction: Dry working standards at Room temperature under N 2 gas stream (<2 psi).

steps.title: Transfer concentrated sulfuric acid from the...
steps.instruction: Transfer concentrated sulfuric acid from the original package to a 95% ethanol rinsed and air-dried glass reagent bottle for temporary storage.

steps.title: Weigh a 95% ethanol rinsed and...
steps.instruction: Weigh a 95% ethanol rinsed and air-dried glass reagent bottle, and tare the balance

steps.title: Use 5 ml serological pipet to...
steps.instruction: Use 5 ml serological pipet to measure and transfer concentrated sulfuric acid to this vial. The volume of sulfuric acid is several milliliter more than estimated in go to step #6 . Write down the weight of sulfuric acid. Sulfuric acid (mL) Sulfuric acid (g) 1

steps.title: The weight of dichromate required for the 0
steps.instruction: The weight of dichromate required for the 0.15% (w/w) acid-dichromate reagent equals the weight of sulfuric acid multiplied by (0.15/99.85).

steps.title: Weigh dichromate and dissolve it into concentrated sulfuric acid
steps.instruction: Weigh dichromate and dissolve it into concentrated sulfuric acid. Cap the vial and vortex until complete dissolve. Dichromate (g) 1

steps.title: Label two 12 mL vials with...
steps.instruction: Label two 12 mL vials with “+ Blank” and “- Blank”. "-Blank" is 0 ug GTP. "+Blank" is the reference of absorbance.

steps.title: Prepare boiling water bath on hot...
steps.instruction: Prepare boiling water bath on hot plate, place a vial rack in the water bath

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.5 mL of acid-dichromate reagent to \textcolor{red}{standards, +Blank, –Blank} and some of the samples . Cap and vortex right after. Use 5 mL glass serological pipet, fill to "4 mL", dispense 0.5 mL .

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.2 mL of acid-dichromate reagent to (1) \textcolor{red}{sample} \textcolor{red}{blanks} , (2) some of the \textcolor{red}{samples} . Cap and vortex right after. Use 5 mL glass serological pipet, fill to "4 mL", dispense 0.2 mL .

steps.title: Keep reaction vials in boiling water for 00:15:00
steps.instruction: Keep reaction vials in boiling water for 00:15:00 .

steps.title: Cool vials to Room temperature in the fumehood
steps.instruction: Cool vials to Room temperature in the fumehood

steps.title: Prepare 0
steps.instruction: Prepare 0.2 g/ml sodium sulphite solution Weigh 0.2 g sodium sulphite in a 2 ml microtube. Add 1 mL MilliQ water into the tube. Vortex

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.125 mL o MilliQ (1 mL + 125 uL by pipet) to those reacted with 0.5 mL dichromate. Cap immediately and vortex.

steps.title: Add 450 µL MilliQ to those reacted with 0
steps.instruction: Add 450 µL MilliQ to those reacted with 0.2 mL dichromate. Cap immediately and vortex.

steps.title: Cool vials to room temperature
steps.instruction: Cool vials to room temperature.

steps.title: Add 25 µL 0
steps.instruction: Add 25 µL 0.2 g/ml sodium sulphite solution to the “+Blank” vial. Vortex.

steps.title: Reverse pipetting, load 200 µL r reactant into microtiter plate
steps.instruction: Reverse pipetting, load 200 µL r reactant into microtiter plate.

steps.title: Subtract absorbance of "+Blank" from the absorbance of standards
steps.instruction: Subtract absorbance of "+Blank" from the absorbance of standards.

steps.title: Plot the resulted absorbance versus mass of GTP (ug)
steps.instruction: Plot the resulted absorbance versus mass of GTP (ug).

steps.title: Calculate the mass of lipids by...
steps.instruction: Calculate the mass of lipids by using the standard curve and the absorbance, including sample blanks and samples. GTP_{ug} =(Abs-intercept)/slope

steps.title: Lipids_{ug}=GTP_{ug}*(0
steps.instruction: Lipids_{ug}=GTP_{ug}*(0.5/V_{dichromate}) , where V_{dichromate} is the actual volume of dichromate reagent added.

steps.title: Lipids_{ug/filter}=(V_1/V_2)*Lipids_{ug} , where V_1 is the...
steps.instruction: Lipids_{ug/filter}=(V_1/V_2)*Lipids_{ug} , where V_1 is the volume of chloroform added into the dried extract, V_2 is the volume of extract used in the assay.

steps.title: Prepare two vial racks, one is for those with 0
steps.instruction: Prepare two vial racks, one is for those with 0.5 mL dichromate, the other is for those with 0.2 mL dichromate. Label the racks to avoid adding MilliQ in wrong volume.

steps.title: For sample blanks, add 5 mL chloroform and transfer 4
steps.instruction: For sample blanks, add 5 mL chloroform and transfer 4.5 mL to a new vial. Discard the remaining extract.

steps.title: Dry the extract in the new vial for assay
steps.instruction: Dry the extract in the new vial for assay.

steps.title: Store the remaining extract -80 °C (for phospholipids assay)
steps.instruction: Store the remaining extract -80 °C (for phospholipids assay).

steps.title: Prepare sodium sulphite 0
steps.instruction: Prepare sodium sulphite 0.2 g/ml In a 2 mL microtube, add 0.4 g sodium sulphite and 2 mL MilliQ, vortex.
